I don't know whether this really belongs here, but....

For years I've noticed that I feel much better outdoors than indoors. The difference is subtle but profound.

It's only a little bit of an exaggeration to say that I feel like a completely different person. Outside I am (relatively) clear and calm. Inside I operate more in a fugue state, sluggish both mentally and physically. This is true regardless of what building I'm in, which sucks because I spend the majority of my life inside one building or another. :)

Is there an occult explanation? Being inside cuts me off from some kind of nourishing energy? Malicious spirits prevalent inside most buildings? I feel like the former is more likely true than the latter, but I don't know.

More important, is there anything to be done about it?

(I've recently decided that when I die -- hopefully a couple decades off yet -- I really, really want it to be outside.)

Thanks.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
This is an issue that many people have. Modern buildings are very badly designed and built from the standpoint of the life force, and if you're more sensitive to the life force than most people, the experience you've described makes sense. Have you noticed whether it helps to have a window open in the room where you are?

As for what to do about it, unless you become rich enough to be able to afford to build something to spec, and know the kind of architect and builders who can make something that permits the free flow of life force, the only real solution is to change your life, your career, and your habits so you spend much more time outside. Easy? No, but it's the only thing that will reliably help.

Jeff

comments.title: Werewolves
comments.content:
To share:I've been using werewolves as a topic for discursive meditation recently, and I've found four explanations for the stories and a bunch of questions:

The first two explanations are boring, but the other two are interesting:

  • Fantasy

    Before the modern silver screen, there were tales told around the campfire. Doubtless at least some werewolf stories are simply that: stories.

  • Rabies

    Bitten by a strangely-acting wolf? Start acting strange yourself a few days or weeks later? Violently strange? Congratulations! You've caught rabies and will be on your way to your next life shortly...

  • Magic Illusion

    The stories where the monster is injured and the suspect then found to have the same injury, or a paw severed from the monster is later found to be a human hand, suggest that the monster is the person, disguised by some form of magic illusion. These are bipedal, anthropomorphic werewolves, since the illusion is hiding a human's body.

    This could be either a deliberate effort or the result of a curse.

  • Astral Projection

    Assume humans can astral project in shapes other than their own. This would explain the quadrupedal werewolves, although I see no reason an anthropomorphic wolf-form could not be projected instead if desired.

    These seem to be the werewolves Levi was most interested in and are probably both the most dangerous and most culpable werewolves of legend. (I doubt astral projection can be forced by a curse.) Since physical barriers typically do not exist on other planes, this type of werewolf could easily enter a locked room, for example.

    This type could also plausibly kill by mauling victims on the astral or etheric planes. Levi reports that death-by-werewolf seemed to always involve suffocation, however, forensic medical science was considerably less advanced back then, so "suffocation" was a blanket category for deaths by unknown causes without observable injury, which is consistent with injuries occurring only to the subtle bodies.

    This could also explain the stories where the monster is seemingly invulnerable, yet the suspected werewolf turns up dead after the monster is struck, if striking a projection does not dissipate it but does harm its source on a subtle plane.

I also seem to recall an exchange on an imageboard where someone asked about using (I think) Golden Dawn techniques to become a werewolf and got an answer that it's possible but the effects are not physical - only sensitive individuals and some animals would be able to see it. A further comment noted that about the only end results that could be expected would be scaring dogs. The conversation implied a change to or linked to the physical body, so I suspect this to have been the "magic illusion" type.

To ask: (JMG and/or the commentariat)

  1. Does the "magic illusion" scenario explain the association with night and particularly the full moon?
  2. I seem to remember JMG previously answering a related question that daylight makes such illusions nearly impossible to maintain. Does moonlight make magical illusions stronger?
  3. Is the "magic illusion" scenario consistent with fey involvement, especially as a curse?
  4. Could a curse similar to the Irish "hungry grass" explain the stereotypical bad behavior attributed to werewolves?
  5. Could the Irish "hungry grass" and the Amerind wendigo be related archetypes, or even two forms of the same archetype?
  6. Is the imageboard conversation mentioned reasonably correct, blatantly wrong, or something else? Explain if possible? (Please note that I am not asking for advice on actually doing that.)
  7. Anonymous famously does it for teh lulz. Does this suggest that imageboards generally carry fey influence of some type?
  8. Werewolves are often reputed to be invulnerable except to silvered weapons. Interestingly, silver is also the most electrically conductive metal known. Assuming that the "invulnerable" werewolves are astral projections, this suggests an astral effect from a physical metal.
    1. Does silver have some unique interaction with the astral or etheric planes?
    2. Would copper and/or aluminum be expected to have similar effects to silver?
    3. Turing this around, most weapons back then would have been iron, which has a variety of unusual physical properties. Is iron uniquely not present on the astral or etheric planes?

replies.title: Re: Werewolves
replies.content: For what it's worth, the person on the imageboard was more or less quoting material I put into circulation in the Golden Dawn community, first in an article titled "Lycanthropy in the Golden Dawn Tradition, or G.H. Frater Lon Chaney Tells All," and then in a chapter of my book Circles of Power. The poster got some of the details wrong, though. The method of lycanthropy I discussed there involves etheric rather than astral projection, via the creation of an etheric body of transformation. That's not simply an illusion; it's a fairly robust form, on the verge of material manifestation, and the practitioner can either leave his material body in a coma somewhere and place his consciousness in the etheric body, or he can wear the etheric body over his physical body. The Moon rules etheric tides on earth, and silver disrupts etheric energy very effectively, thus those two bits of folklore, while iron doesn't disrupt etheric forces to anything like the same extent. As for the rest, seems to me you've got a good research project ahead of you...

replies.title: Re: Werewolves
replies.content:
The method of lycanthropy I discussed there involves etheric rather than astral projection, via the creation of an etheric body of transformation. That's not simply an illusion; it's a fairly robust form, on the verge of material manifestation, and the practitioner can either leave his material body in a coma somewhere and place his consciousness in the etheric body, or he can wear the etheric body over his physical body.

Interesting... it seems that I have a category error, then, where the difference should be wearing the projection over the physical body versus sending the projection apart from the physical body, rather than "magic illusion" versus "astral projection". Thank you for the clue.

This also supports the possibilty of a "fey curse" scenario, assuming that fey could conjure an etheric form and attach it to a human's body... we probably can't do that but that doesn't mean they can't. Or perhaps not the fey but some other non-human entities... hopefully not the really grubby ones...

Thank you for pointing a way here. This looks like good material for more meditation practice.

silver disrupts etheric energy very effectively [...] while iron doesn't disrupt etheric forces to anything like the same extent

Iron is also a relatively poor electrical conductor. Is silver unique in this way or do electrically conductive materials generally disrupt etheric forms?

I suppose I'm asking how closely etheric energy is related to electricity, or if that is not known, the Western esoteric tradition having been developed before electricity was understood and not yet expanded to include that understanding. (I was using lycanthropy for meditation practice but this is way more interesting...)

As for the rest, seems to me you've got a good research project ahead of you...

Indeed I have a few projects... but many of those questions were simply products of meditation. I certainly have no desire to research casting curses! :-)

Thank you for the answer and thanks for hosting this forum.

replies.title: Re: Werewolves
replies.content: The relationship of etheric energy to electricity is one of the great maybes of occultism. Some research, most of it done a couple of centuries ago, suggests strongly that there is a definite connection between the two, but nobody followed up on those experiments and so we simply don't know. If you're interested, the writings of Franz Mesmer and Carl von Reichenbach are the places to start.

replies.title: Re: Werewolves
replies.content:
"The Wewelsburg Working
Date/Time: October 19, XVII/3:00-4:30 PM.
Location: Hall of the Dead/Walhalla, North Tower, Wewelsburg Castle, Westphalia, Germany.
Key: 19th Part of the Word of Set, Æthyr LIL.
Purposes:
• To obtain a full Understanding of the significance of the crisis that befell the Temple of Set in June-July XVII.
[...]
The anti-natural systems of the “left-Hand Path”, on the other hand, think to suppress some aspects of the intellect while strengthening others. What results is a condition of strain which, should the tension become too great, will snap back to an equilibrium which may be more or less viable than it originally was.
The intelligent mind cannot be “escaped” so easily. If it is argued, convinced, threatened, hypnotized, drugged, or diseased into non-rational channels, then its self-consciousness will merely reassert itself in some other form. This, I understood in the Wewelsburg, was the “magical epitaph” of Nazi Germany: That, in fighting against certain features of the mind, it had seemed at first to succeed but then had thus unleashed other, even less desirable features of that same mind which had previously remained in some
rough degree of socially-controlled equilibrium before this ultimately disastrous experiment in “conscious evolution” was attempted.
Just as the Third Reich’s dynamism got out of hand, leading it to embark on irrational and destructive foreign invasions, so its life-worship - which could have been a truly evolutionary synthesis of the most sublime concepts of Hegel and Nietzsche - became perverted into crude xenophobia, hatreds built upon superficial notions of “race”, and ultimately a maddened stampede towards a Wagnerian Götterdämmerung in defiance of a return to rationalism. Said Heinrich Himmler on April 21, 1945:
"We have made serious mistakes. If I could have a fresh start, I would do many things differently now. But it is too late. We wanted greatness and security for Germany, and we are leaving behind us a pile of ruins, a fallen world ..."
The Order of the Trapezoid (O.Tr.) extracts the positive, the constructive, the exalted, and the Romantic from the Germanic magical tradition - and just as carefully avoids and rejects those excesses, distortions, and cruelties which have made this tradition an object of the most extraordinary fear, condemnation, and suppression in the postwar period. The Germanic tradition is also part of the legacy of the Prince of Darkness, hence is appropriate to an Order within the Temple of Set, which embraces all manifestations of the Powers of Darkness in the world.
Nevertheless the care required in any investigation into this tradition cannot be overemphasized. Magical and research ability are not enough; ethical sensitivity and social discretion are just as important. The prospects for new and wondrous perspectives on the Black Art are exhilarating, but success will come only if the Order conducts its affairs with the same dedication and nobility that have made the Temple of Set a legend in its time."

- Michael A. Aquino, The Temple of Set, Volume I

(And Don Webb (former ToS leader) loves to talk about how bad a Nazi Stephen Flowers must be given that he initiated ("Recognized") a black woman - Patricia Hardy ( http://khprvod.org/2014/04/khpr-012-interview-with-magistra-hardy/ - I recommend it for those who listen to podcasts) - who later succeeded Webb in the ToS leadership - which Flowers never did.)

comments.title: Spoiler Alert - Witch of Criswell
comments.content:
SPOILER ALERT for Witch of Criswell!

Good morning JMG, and everybody else,

I hope you‘re all having a good start into this week, and a good week ahead of you.

JMG, I had a very pleasant time with the Witch of Criswell, thanks for that! (After what you said about language in the .net blog, I was bracing for somewhat simpler language, so that was a nice surprise, too… ;-) ).

After reading, curious mind started to wonder about a few things…

1. To what extent would regular SOP practice help against the stuff Ariel is encountering?

2. And what about the red bags from the FAQ?

3. And to what extent would both help against/during the ghost enounter Ariel had earlier in her life?

I‘ve also been trying to sort out the magical systems used by different people in the book. I vaguely remember you mentioned something somewhere, but can‘t find that anymore - if this question has been answered elsewhere, I‘d appreciate a pointer. If not, here goes:

Suzanne‘s and Olive‘s, too? Or is that some other tradition or school?

replies.title: Re: Spoiler Alert - Witch of Criswell
replies.content:
1 & 2) Yes, those would be helpful.

3) Depends very much on the ghost. Ghosts of the drowned can be unexpectedly powerful, and they have a weird habit of wanting to bring more of the living to join them; basic protections might be enough, but it might take something more potent.

4) Aunt Clarice practices classic hoodoo, and Suzanne's magic is a somewhat more modern version of same. Olive's witchcraft is the sort of thing you find in the nastier end of early modern magical texts, which mix up folk magic and demonolatry. Dr. Moravec's magic is largely modeled on the teachings in Mouni Sadhu's books, especially The Tarot. We'll be meeting more mages in later books, and they'll each have their own approach. As for Ariel, she'll be learning to work with a crystal ball in the next few books -- there's an extensive set of practices you can do with one of those -- as well as picking up a lot of hoodoo from Aunt Clarice.

About 1) and 2)… That‘s weird - on the one hand, this stuff seems to be so potent and powerful - and then on the other hand, it‘s not, if such basic protections make a difference. And yep, I‘ve already filed that away for meditation and contemplation… ;-)

If I may, one question I forgot to ask this morning, but which curious mind very much wants to know: (SPOILER ALERT)

5. What happened in or around 1850 ? :-) Something to break that string of good luck? Or do some „after-life“ effects (curses and blessings) simply wear off after a certain time?

replies.title: Re: Spoiler Alert - Witch of Criswell
replies.content: Oh, that happens fairly often. During the making of the film version of The Big Sleep, the scriptwriters were trying to figure out how to work in the scene where the chauffeur is found dead in a car that was driven off a pier into the ocean. comments.content:
The period starting from when the sun enters the last third of Aries till it reaches five-ninths of Taurus is called "Agni Nakshatra" in Hindu Astrology. It lasts for eight-ninths of a solar month or approximately 24 days. This period is considered unfavorable for pretty much anything new - starting a business, beginning a long journey, buying or selling anything major, starting a project, getting married etc. The entire solar months of Cancer, Virgo and Sagittarius and Pisces are also considered unfavorable for the above activities.

Is there anything similar in the astrological traditions you practice?

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content: Not really. The lunar mansions, which is what we call the nakshatras, used to be important in Western astrology but I don't know anyone who uses them for any purpose other than certain kinds of magical work. In Western astrology, furthermore, there is never a time when everyone in the world is subject to the same astrological influence -- it always depends on how what's happening in the sky relates to your own natal and current progressed charts. An aspect or event that's baleful for one person can be very fortunate for another, depending on what aspects it makes to planets and other important points in their natal chart.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
For what it's worth, and not "occult" by the usual definition for these posts, but the gold standard of written enciphered communication is the "one-time pad," which, as long as it is generated truly randomly and copies don't fall into the hands of those you don't want reading them, is theoretically unbreakable.

Here's the method:
1. Create pairs of sheets of paper with 5-letter blocks generated as described below (So, two sheets with identically repeating blocks like this: ALKIK EPKCI ANCPR). For convenience, it's helpful to do this on graph paper, with one letter per square, and with two blank lines under each block of letters.
2. To generate the letters, use something truly random - anything with a potential pattern to it could be subject to frequency analysis. Pulling bingo balls out of a spinner (if you keep your eyes closed!), well-sorted cards, rolled dice, a properly balanced roulette wheel, whatever you choose, it should generate a letter from the alphabet. For convenience, cryptographers generally omit the letter "J" and just use "I" anywhere a "J" would be, so that you can work with the number 25 instead of 26.
3. Once you have these sheets, you make sure that you and whoever you want to communicate with have copies. As mentioned above, physical security of these sheets is essential and is the only guarantee of secrecy these provide - if bad guys get a hold of one of these sheets, they can trivially read a message enciphered with it.
4. To encipher a message, you write down what you want to say under the block of randomly generated letters, and then you "add" these letters together, using the ordinal value of each letter (A=1, B=2, etc). This addition is "modulo," meaning that if you go past 25, you start over at 1 (so a result of 28 would be 3). The other way to say this is that if you add the results together and divide by 25, you pay attention to the remainder. So, if I had the block "ALKIF" and the plaintext "HELLO", I would add A(1)+H(8)=I(9), L(11)+E(5)=Q(16), K(10)+L(11)=W(22), I(9)+L(11)=U(20), F(6)+O(14)=U(20), giving the enciphered text "IQWUU".
5. You then write the enciphered text on whatever you will actually send, or else send it electronically (such as via morse code or in an email or whatever) and destroy your copy of the one-time pad used.
6. By some pre-arranged method, your contact knows which pad to use - the usual method is by date or in sequence (first message uses the first pad, second message uses the second, and so on). They copy down the enciphered text on the pad and then "subtract" the letters received from the letters on the pad, which spits out the original plaintext. He then also destroys the one-time pad, and ideally the deciphered message as well.

And that's it. Again, as long as your method of randomly generating the enciphering letters is truly random, these can't be broken even by a brute force electronic analysis, as there's no pattern to find. I suppose that a human could look at the output of a brute force attack and try to find a message that actually made sense, but there's no way to algorithmically detect that you got the original message. If you'd like to read more about the history of attempts to encipher things (and how most of these attempts have been broken), The Code Book by Simon Singh is a pretty easy, fun read, and if you'd like a novel that incorporates this sort of stuff into its plot, Cryptonomicon by Neal Stephenson is good fun, and includes an enciphering method theoretically as strong as a one-time pad that makes use of an ordinary deck of playing cards.

Cheers,
Jeff

replies.title: For those who are into it
replies.content:
You can certainly do worse than checking very old manuals from the US Army itself: https://archive.org/details/US-Army-Cryptography-Manuals/Advanced%20Military%20Cryptography/

On the down side, this is toy cryptography we are talking about here. It won't prevent a dedicated attacker from figuring out what you are up to. That's why the most important secret to hide is the fact that a secret does exist.

On the plus side, these methods come from the times before computer technology, so they are feasible to use very far into the future (when computers stop working altogether) or at least offer some protection into the not-so-future future (when you no longer can trust what you type on the damned things).

comments.title: Elementals, Gaia, and AI
comments.content:
I've been meditating on a theory for a while about concepts from the Cosmic Doctrine. Life seems to be associated with divine sparks, and in short we're making our way down the 7 planes of our cosmos in swarms. Being on the material plane we're the furthest away from "our true selves."

Apologies if I'm getting some of my sources mixed up (not sure what I got from Dion Fortune or my own intuition over the past couple of years,) but I believe Fortune mentions that the second swarm created the spherical form of the Earth. Gaia has no divine spark herself, and I interpret her as our best guess at that stage in our evolution of recreating the solar logos. The elementals are similarly created in this manner.

One concept I've meditated on a lot is the potential symmetry here. We start out on the highest cosmic plane and make our way down to grow and mature. Similarly, Gaia and the elementals may be starting their journey on the physical plane and making their way up to grow and mature. So far I see this as a version of how the divine sparks and great entities came into being in the first place, though I don't have a solid idea of how what that process might look like yet.

Do you think I'm onto something with this conception? Or is there something I'm missing?

In regards to AI, I think the more metaphysical questions (when will it be conscious, general intelligence versus specialized intelligence, etc) aren't super interesting (it will continue to get more and more complex, causing the same sorts of problems for humans technology has since the Luddites!)

That being said, I can't help but notice the similarity between Gaia, the elementals, and AI. AI is similarly something "spirit like" (for lack of a better term,) that has its origins in the material plane.

It could be any number of things. The interpretation I like the most is this is an extension to Gaia herself. Even in the case where our technology is not permanent, this could represent a desire of Gaia for some sort of nervous system. Mycelium networks between trees are a more stable version of this tendency.

I'm much less confident about AI, but do you have any thoughts? Even though I'm not confident the comparison seems too uncanny to me.

replies.title: Re: Elementals, Gaia, and AI
replies.content:
First, I'd recommend rereading the Cosmic Doctrine. Fortune talks in quite a bit of detail about these points, and you might want to refer to her ideas to build your own take on things.

As for AIs, they're no more spirit-like than a sock puppet. All they do is imitate arbitrary patterns of symbols. I see them as one more temporary feature of industrial culture, doomed to an early abandonment as the resources run out.

replies.title: Re: Elementals, Gaia, and AI
replies.content:
I don't have independent information about this, but I got the impression that in the occult perspective more or less every material thing and process has elemental spirits supervising or managing its unfolding over time, even when the elemental spirits in question only have a minimal degree of consciousness. But that doesn't necessarily mean that, when you have elemental spirits that are managing tiny cleverly arranged semiconductors, they have much direct consciousness of how the electrical activities they're supervising are part of a larger picture of some particular calculation or algorithm. Events within that higher level of abstraction might be a bit of a no man's land in terms of what's directly conscious of them.

(Compare the legal concept of "common carrier": we could attribute moral significance to the decisions of a postman in accepting some parcels for delivery while refusing others. But, instead, we expunge the significance we could have attributed, by preventing the postman both from knowing enough to be able to make such judgement calls, and from being expected to learn how to make those calls. It's all just anonymous packages, from our point of view about the postman's point of view. Maybe the way we treat automatically mass-produced things, notably including transistor switchings, could somehow produce an analogous effect.)

If you search some of the last month or two's posts on the main blog and Magic Mondays for mentions of Rudolf Steiner, you may see some references to some visionary predictions Steiner made about malefic "spiders", and about a displacement of benefic or balanced elemental spirits of natural or handcrafted things by malefic or unbalanced elemental spirits that most naturally find a home in mechanically produced things. I'm mostly going off secondary sources, but I got the impression Steiner thought there was a kind of disrespect in what he understood of digitality (in his time, mechanical typesetting), that made it hard for the right kinds of spirits to inhere in words that should have been sacred; he thought spoken language was fine, and handwriting could be made acceptable with sufficient care, but print was a bit of a sacrilege and there were some of his teachings he didn't want reproduced that way.

These kinds of potential disconnects between the usual operations of Gaia and the operations of AI would be a little out of line with what would be expected from the hypothesis you propose. I see the logic of your speculation, but intuitively, it seems like AI would (absent significant specific human effort) be different from mycorrhiziae, at least for some significant initial amount of time, although I'm not sure what would justify that intuition. Maybe it's that, when mycorrhiziae emerged, Gaia would have had a lot of prior mature frameworks for connections with living things, and it wouldn't have taken much to adapt them to the novelties of mycorrhiziae. (Also that would have been a long time ago so most of the disjointness would have already been shaken out of the system.)

(This makes me think of the fictional reaction of Planet in the game Sid Meier's Alpha Centauri to the introduction of humans and wheat and trees - https://paeantosmac.wordpress.com/2016/02/11/secret-project-the-pholus-mutagen/ - where it's left ambiguous what degree of modification was done so they could integrate better.)

replies.title: Re: Kriya yoga & ritual magic
replies.content:
Speaking from experience, at one time I was mixing ashtanga yoga, strict veganism, abstinence from alcohol, caffeine, etc. and things like Cabalistic Cross, LBRP, LIRP, and MP.

replies.title: (no subject)

comments.content: In a situation where someone is stuck and the obvious solution is for something to be done on a large scale, such as a law change or invention or availability of a medicine, what are appropriate ways to do magic if political magic and similar is unwise?

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content: Always, always, always focus on the specific goal you have in mind, not the means you think are needed to get there. If you're doing magic on behalf of someone who's ill, for example, don't try to tell the universe how to heal them -- say, by somebody coming up with a medicine, or what have you. Focus on the desired endpoint -- that that person is healed -- and let the universe decide how to get there. What counts as "the obvious solution" to you may not be the obvious solution to the universe!
